ReadRangeX.note: Self-closing tag. Modern Excel Read Range (used inside ExcelApplicationCard scope). (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
ReadRangeX.description: Read Range X activity from the excel category.
ReadRangeX.use_when: User wants to interact with Excel using Read Range X.

WriteRangeX.note: Self-closing tag. Modern Excel Write Range (used inside ExcelApplicationCard scope). (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
WriteRangeX.description: Write Range X activity from the excel category.
WriteRangeX.use_when: User wants to interact with Excel using Write Range X.

AutoFillX.note: Self-closing tag. Auto-fills a range in an Excel workbook. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
AutoFillX.description: Auto Fill X activity from the excel category.
AutoFillX.use_when: User wants to interact with Excel using Auto Fill X.

FilterX.note: Self-closing tag. Applies a filter to an Excel range. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
FilterX.description: Filter X activity from the excel category.
FilterX.use_when: User wants to interact with Excel using Filter X.

FilterPivotTableX.note: Self-closing tag. Filters a pivot table in Excel. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
FilterPivotTableX.description: Filter Pivot Table X activity from the excel category.
FilterPivotTableX.use_when: User wants to interact with Excel using Filter Pivot Table X.

InsertExcelChartX.note: Self-closing tag. Inserts a chart into an Excel worksheet. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
InsertExcelChartX.description: Insert Excel Chart X activity from the excel category.
InsertExcelChartX.use_when: User wants to interact with Excel using Insert Excel Chart X.

ExcelApplicationCard.note: ExcelApplicationCard is a scope activity with ActivityAction body requiring Studio configuration to open/attach to workbooks.
ExcelApplicationCard.description: Excel Application Card (wizard-only) — must be configured through UiPath Studio's interactive wizard.
ExcelApplicationCard.use_when: Do not auto-generate Excel Application Card. Direct the user to author it in UiPath Studio.

ExcelProcessScopeX.note: ExcelProcessScopeX is a scope activity controlling an Excel process instance; requires Studio configuration.
ExcelProcessScopeX.description: Excel Process Scope X (wizard-only) — must be configured through UiPath Studio's interactive wizard.
ExcelProcessScopeX.use_when: Do not auto-generate Excel Process Scope X. Direct the user to author it in UiPath Studio.

ExcelForEachRowX.note: ExcelForEachRowX uses a dual-argument ActivityAction body (CurrentRow, CurrentIndex) requiring Studio configuration.
ExcelForEachRowX.description: Excel For Each Row X (wizard-only) — must be configured through UiPath Studio's interactive wizard.
ExcelForEachRowX.use_when: Do not auto-generate Excel For Each Row X. Direct the user to author it in UiPath Studio.

ForEachSheetX.note: ForEachSheetX uses a dual-argument ActivityAction body (CurrentSheet, CurrentIndex) requiring Studio configuration.
ForEachSheetX.description: For Each Sheet X (wizard-only) — must be configured through UiPath Studio's interactive wizard.
ForEachSheetX.use_when: Do not auto-generate For Each Sheet X. Direct the user to author it in UiPath Studio.

UpdateChartX.note: UpdateChartX wraps an ActivityAction body for chart update operations requiring Studio configuration.
UpdateChartX.description: Update Chart X (wizard-only) — must be configured through UiPath Studio's interactive wizard.
UpdateChartX.use_when: Do not auto-generate Update Chart X. Direct the user to author it in UiPath Studio.

ChangeDataRangeModification.note: ChangeDataRangeModification has no resolvable CLR namespace and cannot be emitted without Studio wizard.
ChangeDataRangeModification.description: ChangeDataRangeModification (wizard-only) — must be configured through UiPath Studio's interactive wizard.
ChangeDataRangeModification.use_when: Do not auto-generate ChangeDataRangeModification. Direct the user to author it in UiPath Studio.
